AI1v-Phase-1.title: **Channel unit auto-inference, deterministic floor.** New `ChannelUnitInferenceService` (in-tree CDI bean) maps a channel `field` name to a QUDT canonical IRI via three resolution tiers: SUFFIX (`_mm`, `_N`, `_bar`, …), PREFIX_HEURISTIC (`tc_`, `pc_`, `j1_…j7_`, …), WELDING_CAP (`CM_I` → Ampere, `W1_U` → Volt, …). Wired into the channel-creation write path inside `TimeseriesSemanticDualWriteService` so every new channel gets a `:SemanticAnnotation` with `propertyIRI='urn:shepard:unit'`, `valueIRI=<QUDT>`, `sourceMode='ai'`, `source='ts-channel-unit-suffix'`, and a tier-derived `confidence` in {1.0, 0.9, 0.85}. Ambiguous fields (`BridgePosition`, `valve_*`, welding-cap `_p` / `_t`) are logged at WARN and skipped.

AI1v-Phase-2.title: **LLM resolution of the ambiguous tail.** When `ChannelUnitInferenceService.infer()` returns `Tier.AMBIGUOUS` (≈18% of LUMEN+MFFD channels today: `BridgePosition`, `valve_fuel`, `valve_lox`, welding-cap `_p` / `_t` variants), the AI plugin TEXT capability is invoked with the parent DataObject context (process step, instrument family, sibling channels) to propose a QUDT IRI. Output writes a `:SemanticAnnotation` with `sourceMode='ai'` and a real (non-1.0) `confidence`; UI accept-flip promotes to `collaborative`.

AI1v-Phase-3.title: **SHACL `UNIT_MISMATCH` validator on `ShapesRenderResponseIO`.** View recipes can declare expected units (e.g. Trace3D wants `MilliM` on its x/y/z channels); the SHACL engine cross-checks the channel's `urn:shepard:unit` annotation against the recipe expectation and emits a typed violation when they disagree. Closes the loop on the "unit is just data" critique: once Phase 1+2 populate the annotation, Phase 3 makes it _validatable_.

IX1.title: External-service minimal-instance infrastructure — prefer running **the real service in a minimal Docker container** over synthetic WireMock stubs; stubs only for services with no self-hostable open-source image. Target list and preferred image for each: (a) **Helmholtz Databus + MOSS** (`R1/MB1`) — Apache Jena Fuseki testcontainer (`stain/jena-fuseki`) is the actual SPARQL store Databus uses; MOSS indexer gets a real minimal MOSS module config pointed at it; validates the full SHACL + DCAT round-trip against real SPARQL semantics. (b) **AAS Registry** (`AAS1-reg`) — `eclipsebasyx/aas-environment:2.x` official image; exercises the real BaSyx REST wire shape including `AssetAdministrationShell` registration payloads. (c) **Matrix home server** (`MTX1a`) — `matrixdotorg/synapse:latest` or `conduit` minimal testcontainer; real `/_matrix/client/v3` routes so the notification flow is exercised against actual Matrix protocol. (d) **Open3D sidecar** (`PC1b`, `VID2a`) — real image we build from `infrastructure/sidecar-open3d/`; testcontainered directly via the built image tag. (e) **SA-Python bridge** (`SA1`) — if the bridge is an HTTP microservice we control, testcontainer it directly; SA COM itself is not CI-testable (Windows-only), so the CI test validates the bridge's HTTP contract against a fixture PLY file. (f) **Helmholtz Unhide HKG harvester** (`UH1`) — no official self-hostable harvester image; use a minimal WireMock container (`wiremock/wiremock:3`) that calls back our feed URL and verifies the JSON-LD shape. (g) **DataCite** (`KIP1d`) — no self-hostable Fabrica; CI falls back to `api.test.datacite.org` OR a WireMock stub set for `/dois` CRUD + `/heartbeat`. (h) **ePIC handle service** (`KIP1c`) — no self-hostable image; WireMock stub for `POST /api/handles/{prefix}/{suffix}`. Pattern: `ExternalServiceStack` Quarkus test-resource (`@QuarkusTestResource`) accepts a `services` init-arg; each enum value starts the correct Testcontainer or WireMock map; `infrastructure-local/docker-compose.external-services.yml` exposes the same images for local dev and manual integration runs without test framework overhead.

MFFD-IMPORT-PERF1.title: **Real worker fan-out for `run_source_mode_workers`.** Extract the per-DO loop body (`mffd-import-v15.py` L2495..2598) into a callable `_process_one_source_do(...)` and submit one future per source DO. Bound at 4 concurrent cube3 GETs + 4 dest POSTs per `aidocs/93 §5`. Today the wrapper is a fake fan-out — it spawns the pool, runs `lambda: True` probes, then calls `run_source_mode` SEQUENTIALLY (`mffd-import-v15.py` L2983..2999). `--workers N` does nothing for throughput.

MFFD-IMPORT-PERF2.title: **Lazy enrichment in `iter_data_objects`.** Yield a stub `SourceDO(do_id, name, attrs, ...)` with `file_refs=None / ts_refs=None / sd_refs=None`; fetch refs only when the per-DO `state.is_*_done` skip check would not fire. Today (`mffd-import-v15.py` L707..711) the script fires 3 cube3 GETs per source DO BEFORE the L2504/L2541/L2570 state-skip checks. On a fully-resumed run that's 25 371 wasted WAN round-trips for an 8 457-DO collection.

MFFD-IMPORT-PERF3.title: **Preempt-friendly self-update apply.** After each completed source DO, check `if updater.update_event.is_set(): break` so the script gracefully exits to the `finally`-block self-update path (`mffd-import-v15.py` L4154..4157). Today the updater detects v15.7 at 60-s cadence (137 events) but the apply is gated by `run_source_mode` returning — operator sees "stuck on 15.6" for 2 h 17 min while the loop runs. Observability win, not a throughput win.

MFFD-IMPORT-PERF4.title: **Add `gauge_cube3_inflight_requests` + `gauge_cube3_p95_latency_ms` to the `Telemetry` class.** Today the operator's only signal that the script is "alive" is heartbeat cadence + `counter_dos_processed`. MFFD-IMPORT-PERF5.title: **Workers=N footgun warning at startup.** Until MFFD-IMPORT-PERF1 ships, `--workers 4` is observably slower than `--workers 1` (it pays pool-spawn cost without buying parallelism). PC-LOD1.title: **Point cloud level-of-detail scheme** — large point clouds (AFP PointCloud.csv, LiDAR scans, photogrammetry) need an LOD pipeline so the viewer stays interactive at 10M+ points. Two tiers: (1) server-side tiling via Potree-compatible octree tiles (`shepard-sidecar-potree-convert`; output tiles in Garage S3 keyed by `PointCloudReference.appId`); (2) progressive streaming via `GET /v2/point-cloud-references/{appId}/tiles/{octree-path}` with viewer `screenSpaceError` param. Alternative: COPC (Cloud Optimised Point Cloud) — single-file LOD via HTTP range requests, no tiling server, lower ops complexity. Design decision required (Potree=battle-tested browser perf; COPC=lower infra burden). Quality gate: 50M-point cloud ≥30fps on integrated GPU. Plugin-first: LOD tiling logic belongs in `shepard-plugin-spatial`.

RDK-PARSE-1.title: **`RdkTextScrapeParser` tier-1 — readable strings out of `MFZ.rdk`**. New module `plugins/fileformat-robotics/` (declared as a `FileParserPlugin` per `aidocs/integrations/110-file-format-parser-plugin.md §3`). Decompresses the `.rdk` zlib payload (4-byte custom magic header skipped), walks `[uint32 LE byte-length][UTF-16LE bytes]` records, and classifies each via predicate-specific regex; emits eight SemanticAnnotations on the parent FileReference under `urn:shepard:rdk:*`: `appVersion`, `platform`, `programSource`, `cadRef[]`, `stepRef[]`, `apiEndpoint`, `robotController`, `companionSpatialAnalyzer` (the last when a `<base>.xit`/`.xit64` sibling exists in the same FileContainer).

RDK-PARSE-2.title: **`RdkKinematicExtractor` tier-2 — full kinematic tree out of `MFZ.rdk`.** New sidecar (declared per `feedback_plugins_declare_sidecars.md`) running RoboDK Python API headless: loads station, walks `RDK.Items()`, extracts joints (axes, limits, types), tool frames (TCP), targets (named poses), reference frames (cell base, table, fixture). Emits a single `:DigitalTwinScene` Neo4j entity with `CoordinateFrame` children, each with `:HAS_PARENT_FRAME` edges, plus `:JOINT_*` properties on robot links. Stored under the parent FileReference as a child `:DigitalTwinScene`. Acceptance: MFZ.rdk → 1 :DigitalTwinScene + ≥ 2 robot subtrees (R10, R20) with 6 joints each + named targets for Ply 1–15. Requires RoboDK SDK licence on the sidecar host (operator opt-in via `FILEFORMAT_RDK_ENABLE=true` env). Failsafe: if sidecar unavailable, tier-1 strings remain.

RDK-WIRE-AGGREGATOR-1.title: **Wire `fileformat-robotics` (and the sibling `fileformat-thermography`) into the aggregator + canonical SPI.** Today both modules are standalone (build via `cd plugins/<name> && ../../backend/mvnw test`) because pulling them into `plugins/pom.xml` worsens the Quarkus/Jandex `CompositeIndex.getClassByName` hang in the backend. When that hang clears (root-caused per `aidocs/agent-findings/jandex-hang.md`): (1) add modules to `plugins/pom.xml` `<modules>`; (2) introduce the canonical `de.dlr.shepard.spi.fileparser.FileParserPlugin` interface in the backend module; (3) replace the local shim interfaces in both modules with `@Override`s against the canonical SPI; (4) add `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.spi.fileparser.FileParserPlugin` descriptors pointing at `RdkTextScrapeParser` + `OTvisParser`; (5) wire `FileContainerService.upload(...)` to dispatch via `FileParserRegistry`; (6) plumb `ParseContext.siblingFiles()` from the FileContainer DAO listing.

RDM-004.title: **Investigate empty Provenance panel on freshly-touched DOs.** Walk surfaced: Provenance expansion-panel renders correctly with READ/MAP/EDIT/ADD/DELETE filter chips but shows "no matching provenance events yet" even after the alice session just READ the DataObject. SCENEGRAPH-REST-1.title: **`/v2/scene-graphs` REST surface — read + write the DigitalTwinScene.** Backend exposes the `:DigitalTwinScene` graph (produced by RDK-PARSE-2 or hand-built) as a REST resource: `GET /v2/scene-graphs/{appId}` returns the frame tree (JSON-LD or plain JSON), `GET …/export.urdf` returns URDF XML, `GET …/export.usd` returns a 503 stub (queued under ISAAC-USD-EXPORT-1), `POST /v2/scene-graphs` creates an empty scene, `POST /v2/scene-graphs/{appId}/frames` adds a frame, `PATCH /v2/scene-graphs/{appId}/frames/{frameAppId}` mutates a single frame's transform + parent + kind, `DELETE /v2/scene-graphs/{appId}/frames/{frameAppId}` removes a subtree, `POST/DELETE /v2/scene-graphs/{appId}/joints[/…]` register / remove joints. Mutations generate `:Activity` with PROV1j `sourceMode`/`agentId` from `X-AI-Agent` and a supplementary `:WAS_DERIVED_FROM` edge to the previous activity for the same scene. Seven MCP tools (`scene_graph_get`, `_create`, `_add_frame`, `_patch_frame`, `_delete_frame`, `_register_joint`, `_delete_joint`, `_export_urdf`). URDF exporter emits `<link>` per frame + `<joint>` with axis + limits; visual / collision / inertial blocks queued for URDF-WEBVIEW-1 phase 2. Frontend placeholder at `/scene-graphs/[appId]` (placeholder kit); real graph browser UI tracked as `SCENEGRAPH-REST-1-UI`. Auth: `@Authenticated` only — the scaffold has no owning-collection pointer; `SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1` row tracks the eventual permission anchor. **`UrdfExporterTest` (6) + `SceneGraphServiceTest` (12).**

SCENEGRAPH-REST-1-UI.title: **Real graph browser UI for `/v2/scene-graphs/{appId}` — replaces the placeholder shipped with SCENEGRAPH-REST-1.** A Vue page rendering the frame tree (vis-network or dagre layout per the Trace3D pattern), per-joint edit panel (slider + axis editor), inline URDF preview via URDF-WEBVIEW-1 once that's wired against `/v2/scene-graphs/{appId}/export.urdf`, and a transform-pad editor for frame x/y/z + rpy. Pair with `useAdvancedMode` to show raw scalar inputs in advanced mode and a 3D gizmo in basic. Acceptance: navigate to `/scene-graphs/<appId>` of the MFFD KR210 scene, scroll the tree without lag at 100+ frames, edit a frame's translation via PATCH and see the URDF preview re-render.

STAGE1.title: **Per-user data dump / staging area** — every authenticated user gets a personal staging space (a private, draft-shaped Collection or a sibling concept) where they can drop files, archives, raw exports, or scratch data without committing to a curated Collection yet. AI analysis plugins (FVF on micrographs, anomaly detection on TS, m4i auto-extraction on PDFs, channel-unit inference) fire against staged data automatically when an AI capability is registered. The user reviews results, then **promotes** items into shared Collections — staged items either move (one-shot) or remain as the read-only origin with provenance edges. The microsections-showcase is the canonical use case: researcher drops `Schliffbilder.7z`, FVF analysis runs per TIF, user accepts results into a sample-series Collection. Pairs with task #26 (ingest data from git) + AI1t (PDF auto-annotation) + AI1v (channel-unit inference) + J2 (JupyterHub plugin); all of those become "things that run inside the staging area" rather than separate top-level integrations. Operator-visible bits: `:UserStagingArea` Neo4j entity (one per `:User`); `GET /v2/me/staging` returns the area; `POST /v2/me/staging/files` uploads; `POST /v2/me/staging/promote` moves items into a Collection with PROV-O `wasDerivedFrom`. UI: a top-bar "Inbox" affordance + a /me/staging page.

T1e.title: `POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects/from-template/{templateAppId}` — server-side DataObject instantiation from a ShepardTemplate. Requires Write on Collection. Gate order: 401 unauth → 404 collection-missing → 403 no-Write → 404 template-missing → 409 retired → 403 not-in-allow-list → create DataObject → stamp `[:CREATED_FROM_TEMPLATE]` edge → 201. Applies `dataobjects[0].attributes` from template body (Jackson `JsonNode.path()` walk, tolerates missing keys). Name resolution: request body `name` field → `dataobjects[0].name` → template name + millis suffix. Empty allow-list = unrestricted.

URDF-MFFD-SHOWCASE-1.title: **MFFD RDK→URDF end-to-end showcase** — `examples/mffd-rdk-urdf-showcase/`. Seeds a Collection demonstrating the full RDK-PARSE-1 + URDF-WEBVIEW-1 phase-1 chain end-to-end against the real MFFD MFZ.rdk fixture: (a) MFZ.rdk uploaded as a FileReference triggers the RDK-PARSE-1 tier-1 auto-scrape (8 `urn:shepard:rdk:*` predicates on the FileReference visible in the semantic-annotations panel); (b) KUKA KR210 L150 URDF + 7 Collada visual meshes uploaded as FileReferences for provenance, also served from `frontend/public/urdf-samples/kr210/` for browser fetch (URDF + meshes pulled from open-source `ros-industrial/kuka_experimental` at `8d9292b04`, Apache-2.0; substitutes for the real KR270 R2700 cell robot per `urdf/SOURCES.md § Caveat`); (c) synthetic 30s × 100 Hz × 6-joint AFP-layup trajectory generated by `trajectory/generate.py` (believable raster sweep motion, joint-limit-respecting), uploaded as a TimeseriesReference with `urn:shepard:urdf:joint = <jointName>` channel annotations so `UrdfChannelPicker` auto-binds zero-click; (d) best-effort VIEW_RECIPE `:ShepardTemplate` POST (skipped with clear log line if not instance-admin). Idempotent `seed.py` mirrors the `examples/mffd-showcase/seed.py` pattern. Three docs: `SHOWCASE.md` (click-walkthrough + honest caveats), `README.md` (runbook + troubleshooting), `urdf/SOURCES.md` (licence + commit hash + KR210→KR270 substitution rationale + refresh procedure). **Routes around** the phase-2 `RdkToUrdfExporter` sidecar (RoboDK SDK licence required, queued) by hand-sourcing the URDF from open-source ROS.

URDF-WEBVIEW-1.title: **in progress (this PR — phase 1: in-tree renderer adapter, plugin extraction deferred).** **`shepard-plugin-vis-urdf` — URDF browser viewer + animator + Foxglove iframe fallback.** **URDF is Shepard's lingua franca for robot kinematics** — ROS RViz, Isaac, MuJoCo, Foxglove, Gazebo all consume it. Plugin ships: (a) `UrdfViewShape` SHACL allow-list extension; (b) `UrdfResolver` returns signed Garage URL for `.urdf` + mesh assets; (c) `UrdfView.vue` using `urdf-loader` (gkjohnson, MIT, npm `urdf-loader`) — Three.js-based, mounts into the shared `<TresCanvas>` from `vis-trace3d`; supports STL/Collada/glTF meshes; (d) **`UrdfAnimator.vue` — trajectory playback**: takes a `:DigitalTwinScene` URDF + a `TimeseriesReference` carrying joint-angle channels (one channel per movable joint, `urn:shepard:urdf:joint` annotation maps channel → joint name; preselection rule applies), drives `urdf-loader`'s per-joint setters against a time cursor. Vuetify play/pause/scrub/speed controls (1x, 0.1x, 10x, reverse). Optional Trace3D TCP-trail overlay shown synchronized in the same `<TresCanvas>`. Frame-rate budget: ≥ 30 FPS at 100 Hz channel rate; downsample with LTTB-vis-toggle if needed (TS-LTTB-VIS-TOGGLE-01 pattern); (e) `UrdfJointPanel.vue` manual Vuetify slider per movable joint (static-view mode); (f) `UrdfRecordButton.vue` — record current animation timeline as a `TimeseriesReference` with the joint-name channel-annotation mapping; (g) Foxglove Studio iframe fallback (`foxglove-studio` Apache-2.0, self-hosted at `cdn.shepard/foxglove/v2/`) for ROS-bag replay against the same URDF — covers the "operator already lives in RViz" use case. Conversion: `RdkToUrdfExporter` sidecar wraps RoboDK Python API → URDF via `robolink.Item.UpdateRobotURDF()` (cell-level info lost; document the lossy boundary in `plugins/vis-urdf/docs/reference.md`). Plugin docs trio. Acceptance: MFZ.rdk → URDF render of R10 in browser; AFP layup recorded joint trajectory plays back at 30+ FPS with TCP-trail synced; static joint sliders work in manual mode; same URDF loads in Foxglove iframe playing a ROS bag.

VID2.title: Camera-to-scene calibration — click-based point-correspondence UI lets a researcher click N paired points (image pixel ↔ CAD/scene world point) to solve the camera extrinsic transform via PnP (OpenCV EPNP+RANSAC; reuses the Open3D sidecar from PC1b). `POST /v2/videos/{videoAppId}/align-to/{cadAppId}` body `{ correspondences: [{imageX, imageY, worldX, worldY, worldZ}], cameraIntrinsics?: [9] }`. User can skip features that are occluded, moving, or unclear — sparse correspondences are fine (min 4, RANSAC-robust above 8). Per-correspondence residual colour feedback (green/yellow/red). Stores `[:CAMERA_ALIGNED_TO { matrix:[16], rmse, correspondenceCount, K:[9], solverAlgorithm, alignedAt }]` on `VideoStream→CadReference`. Unlocks: SB2a CAMERA_FRUSTUM mode (real camera matrix in 3D viewer), 3D annotation → video projection overlay (click annotation → highlight pixel on video), DT1 live camera viewport. Sub-IDs: VID2a (backend PnP + Neo4j edge), VID2b (frontend side-by-side picker UI), VID2c (3D viewer: frustum + projection overlay), VID2d (DT1 camera viewport).
